name: Todoist API
what_it_does: Task management platform API for creating, reading, updating, and completing tasks, projects, labels, and comments in Todoist, with an official MCP server for AI agent task management.
use_cases: Creating and managing tasks from AI agent workflows via the official MCP server
Capturing action items from conversations and routing to Todoist projects
Querying task backlogs for daily standup or planning summaries
Automating task creation from email, calendar, or webhook triggers
Bulk task management and project reorganization workflows
not_for: Enterprise project management with Gantt charts or resource allocation
Team collaboration with real-time co-editing (use Asana or Linear)
Complex workflow automation with conditional logic
best_when: An agent needs to manage personal or team tasks in Todoist, especially for capturing action items, querying task status, or automating task workflows via the MCP server.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade project management with complex dependencies, time tracking, or multi-team resource planning.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user-facing integrations. Personal API token for single-user automation. OAuth scopes include task:add, data:read, data:delete, project:delete. Tokens are per-user.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: 5 personal projects, 5MB uploads, basic features
pricing.notes: API access is available on all plans. Free tier supports basic task operations. Pro/Business plans unlock additional integrations, reminders, and labels.

performance.rate_limits: REST v2: ~450 requests/15 minutes per token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with HTTP status and message. Common errors (invalid token, resource not found) are clear. Error format is consistent across endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Creating a task twice creates two tasks. Agents should check for existing tasks before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API v2 and Sync API v9 are both available with different endpoints and data models — pick one and stick with it
Tasks created via API default to inbox if no project_id specified — always specify project for organization
Rate limit is per user token — shared tokens across multiple agents will exhaust limits quickly
MCP server requires TODOIST_API_TOKEN environment variable
Completed tasks are not returned by default — must explicitly query completed tasks with different endpoint
Due dates use RFC3339 format with optional timezone — missing timezone defaults to user's timezone setting
